summary: Irish playwright known for Pygmalion, Man and Superman, Major Barbara, Saint Joan, Mrs. Warren's Profession, and Arms and the Man. Coined 'Bardolatry.' Will funded a phonetic alphabet. Identified the 'Life Force.'

works.name: Pygmalion
works.description: Shaw's most famous play. Professor Henry Higgins bets Colonel Pickering he can pass the Cockney flower girl Eliza Doolittle off as a duchess. Alfred Doolittle asks for five pounds for his daughter. Mrs. Patrick Campbell debuted the role and caused the longest audience laugh in English stage history with 'Not bloody likely!' The essay 'What Happened Afterwards' explains Eliza married Freddy Eynsford-Hill, not Higgins, and opened a flower shop. Shaw was annoyed that audiences wanted a romance between Higgins and Eliza. Described as a 'Romance in Five Acts.'

clues.clue: Henry Higgins bets he can pass Eliza Doolittle off as a duchess
examples: Henry Higgins tries to teach Eliza Doolittle to pass as an upper class woman

clues.clue: 'Not bloody likely!' — caused longest audience laugh; Mrs. Patrick Campbell
examples: 'Not bloody likely!'

clues.clue: Alfred Doolittle asks for five pounds for his daughter
examples: asks for five pounds in exchange for his daughter

clues.clue: 'What Happened Afterwards' essay — Eliza married Freddy, not Higgins; flower shop
examples: 'What Happened Afterwards' explains Eliza married Freddy

clues.clue: Eliza throws slippers at Higgins
examples: throws slippers at a man

works.name: Man and Superman
works.description: Jack Tanner, author of The Revolutionist's Handbook, marries Ann Whitefield despite resistance. Ann represents the 'Life Force.' Third act is 'Don Juan in Hell' (often performed separately), set in the Spanish Sierra Nevada where the anarchist Mendoza captures them. Cockney chauffeur Henry Straker corrects Tanner about Beaumarchais vs. Voltaire.

clues.clue: Jack Tanner marries Ann Whitefield; she represents the 'Life Force'
examples: Jack Tanner marries Ann Whitefield

clues.clue: Third act 'Don Juan in Hell' — often performed separately
examples: third act is titled 'Don Juan in Hell'

clues.clue: Tanner is author of The Revolutionist's Handbook
examples: The Revolutionist's Handbook

clues.clue: Anarchist Mendoza captures motorists in Sierra Nevada
examples: the anarchist Mendoza... captured by brigands

works.name: Major Barbara
works.description: Barbara Undershaft is conflicted about a donation from her father Andrew Undershaft, a munitions manufacturer. The Undershaft family only bequeaths wealth to foundlings. Adolphus Cusins must reveal his foundling status to inherit the business. A professor of Greek compares Undershaft to Dionysus. Undershaft lists 'respectability and children' among seven deadly sins that only money can cure.

clues.clue: Andrew Undershaft is a munitions manufacturer; family only gives to foundlings
examples: munitions manufacturer Andrew Undershaft

clues.clue: Adolphus Cusins reveals foundling status to inherit the business
examples: Adolphus Cusins reveals his status as a 'foundling'

clues.clue: Undershaft lists seven sins only money can lift
examples: lists 'respectability and children' among the seven sins

works.name: Saint Joan
works.description: About the posthumous canonization of Joan of Arc. Joan identifies the dauphin on sight and causes winds to change. Robert de Baudricourt calls the English 'goddams.' Joan asks 'How long, O Lord, how long?' Shaw called it a 'tragedy without villains.' Describes laying five-dozen eggs as a miracle.

clues.clue: About Joan of Arc — she identifies the dauphin on sight; winds change
examples: a female military commander is able to identify the dauphin of France on sight

clues.clue: Shaw called it a 'tragedy without villains'
examples: this author of a 'tragedy without villains'

clues.clue: Joan says 'there are some very attractive heresies'
examples: 'there are some very attractive heresies'

works.name: Mrs. Warren's Profession
works.description: Vivie Warren discovers her mother runs brothels. Vivie declares 'I don't want a mother; and I don't want a husband.' Vivie takes a job doing actuarial work. One of Shaw's 'Plays Unpleasant.' Ran afoul of censors for overt passages about prostitution.

clues.clue: Vivie learns her mother manages brothels
examples: Vivie is shocked to learn about her mother's brothels

clues.clue: One of Shaw's 'Plays Unpleasant'
examples: Mrs. Warren's Profession, Widowers's Houses, and The Philanderers, a trilogy he grouped under this name

works.name: Arms and the Man
works.description: Set during the Serbo-Bulgarian War. Captain Bluntschli, a 'chocolate-cream soldier,' puts chocolate in his pockets instead of ammunition. Raina Petkoff is the female lead. Adapted into an Oscar Straus operetta The Chocolate Soldier.

clues.clue: Bluntschli the 'chocolate-cream soldier' — chocolate instead of ammunition
examples: Raina Petkoff calls Bluntschli her 'chocolate-cream soldier'

clues.clue: Set during the Serbo-Bulgarian War
examples: used the Serbo-Bulgarian War as the backdrop

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Irish playwright. Coined 'Bardolatry' for excessive Shakespeare worship. Will funded a phonetic alphabet of 40+ letters (only Androcles and the Lion published in it). Wrote 'Quintessence of Ibsenism.' Created the 'Chesterbelloc' portmanteau for Chesterton-Belloc friendship. Also wrote Back to Methuselah, Heartbreak House, Candida, The Dark Lady of the Sonnets, In Good King Charles's Golden Days, The Apple Cart. Mabel Dove wrote a Shaw parody in Ghana.

clues.clue: Coined 'Bardolatry'
examples: 'Bardolatry,' was coined by this playwright

clues.clue: Will funded phonetic alphabet — only Androcles and the Lion published in it
examples: endowed the creation of a phonetic alphabet for English

clues.clue: Wrote 'Quintessence of Ibsenism' — Philistines, Idealists, Realist
examples: divided people into harmless Philistines, dangerous Idealists, and the lone Realist

clues.clue: G.K. Chesterton noted Shaw is 'quick-witted' so 'long-winded'
examples: 'quick-witted that he is long-winded'

comprehensive_summary:
George Bernard Shaw (1856-1950) was an Irish playwright and one of the most important dramatists of the modern era. In quizbowl, he appears extremely frequently, primarily through Pygmalion, Man and Superman, and Major Barbara.

Pygmalion is his most clued work, in which the phonetics professor Henry Higgins bets Colonel Pickering he can pass the Cockney flower girl Eliza Doolittle off as a duchess. Alfred Doolittle memorably asks for five pounds for his daughter. Mrs. Patrick Campbell, who debuted the role of Eliza, caused possibly the longest audience laugh in English stage history with the line 'Not bloody likely!' Shaw was annoyed by romantic readings and wrote the essay 'What Happened Afterwards' to explain that Eliza married Freddy Eynsford-Hill, not Higgins, and opened a flower shop.

In Man and Superman, Jack Tanner marries Ann Whitefield, who embodies the 'Life Force.' The third act, 'Don Juan in Hell,' is often performed separately. Tanner is the author of The Revolutionist's Handbook. The anarchist Mendoza captures Tanner in the Sierra Nevada.

Major Barbara centers on the Undershaft family of munitions manufacturers, who only bequeath wealth to foundlings. Barbara is conflicted about her father Andrew's donation to the Salvation Army. Adolphus Cusins must reveal his foundling status to inherit. Saint Joan is about Joan of Arc's canonization, called a 'tragedy without villains.' Mrs. Warren's Profession features Vivie discovering her mother runs brothels. Arms and the Man features Captain Bluntschli, the 'chocolate-cream soldier,' during the Serbo-Bulgarian War.

Shaw coined 'Bardolatry' and 'Chesterbelloc.' His will funded a phonetic alphabet. He wrote 'Quintessence of Ibsenism' and Back to Methuselah. Also wrote Heartbreak House, Candida, and The Dark Lady of the Sonnets.

Play: Captain Bluntschli puts chocolate in his pockets instead of ammunition during the Serbo-Bulgarian War
